name: New Jersey Community
description_short: New Jersey Community FCU is a member-owned credit union founded in 1954, serving government workers and their families across select NJ towns with competitive rates on savings, auto loans, and mortgages.
description_long:
New Jersey Community Federal Credit Union was established in 1954 to serve government employees working in Camden, New Jersey. Over nearly 70 years, it has grown to serve more than 2,800 members while maintaining its mission as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution. The credit union has expanded its membership eligibility beyond its original government worker focus to include residents of 17 select towns and cities in the Burlington County area, plus family members of existing members regardless of residence.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including share accounts (savings), share certificates (CDs) with terms from 3 months to 5 years, auto loans for new and used vehicles, home equity lines of credit, mortgages, student loans through a Sallie Mae partnership, and primary checking accounts with no monthly fees. Their current advertised rates include 5.24% APY on certificates and competitive auto loan rates. Members can access services through multiple channels: in-person at their office, via PAM (Personal Account Manager) internet banking, PAT (Personal Audio Teller) phone system available 24/7 at 1-800-543-1217, and electronic services for bill payment through PayITe.

What distinguishes New Jersey Community FCU is its geographic and membership specificity combined with personalized service claims. The institution limits membership to residents of specific towns (Cinnaminson, Delran, Eastampton, Evesboro, Evesham, Hainesport, Lumberton, Maple Shade, Marlton, Medford, Medford Lakes, Moorestown, Mount Holly, Mount Laurel, Rancocas Woods, Riverton, and West Moorestown) or family members of existing members. This hyper-local focus positions it as a community-based alternative to large national banks. The credit union emphasizes relationship-based banking and "trusted advice" rather than purely transactional interactions.

For consumers who qualify for membership, New Jersey Community FCU offers legitimate advantages typical of credit unions: no-fee checking, competitive savings rates, and lower-cost loans compared to banks. However, the extremely limited geographic footprint and membership restrictions make it inaccessible to most consumers. The website lacks detailed information about loan terms, APRs for auto loans, mortgage rates, and other specifics that would help prospective members evaluate competitiveness. As a small credit union with 2,800 members, it likely has limited technological sophistication compared to larger financial institutions, though it does offer basic digital banking.
services: Share accounts (savings accounts) with competitive rates
Share certificates (CDs) with terms from 3 months to 5 years
Primary checking accounts with no monthly fees
Auto loans for new and used vehicles
Student loans through Sallie Mae partnership
PAM (Personal Account Manager) internet banking platform
PAT (Personal Audio Teller) 24/7 phone-based account access
Electronic bill payment through PayITe service
pros: No monthly fees on primary checking accounts
Competitive certificate rates up to 5.24% APY for terms of 3 months to 5 years
24/7 account access via PAT (Personal Audio Teller) phone system at 1-800-543-1217
PAM internet banking provides fund transfers, bill pay, and account management online
Student loan offerings through Sallie Mae partnership for educational financing
Member-owned not-for-profit structure means profits return to members as better rates
Expanded membership to family members of existing members regardless of residence
cons: Extremely limited geographic eligibility restricted to 17 specific NJ towns, making it inaccessible to most consumers
Very small institution (2,800 members) with limited resources and likely fewer technology features than larger banks
Website lacks transparent pricing: auto loan APRs, mortgage rates, and most loan terms not disclosed
No physical branch locations mentioned; unclear how many offices serve the membership
Limited product information and missing standard financial disclosures that would help members compare offers
best_for: Residents of Burlington County NJ towns (Moorestown, Marlton, Mount Laurel, etc.) seeking personal relationships with their financial institution
Family members of existing NJ Community FCU members who want to access the credit union regardless of where they live
Government employees and their relatives in the service area looking for community-based lending alternatives to large banks
Savers seeking competitive CD rates and members preferring not-for-profit financial institutions
diagnosis: New Jersey Community FCU is best for residents of specific Burlington County towns or family members of existing members who prioritize personal relationships and community-focused banking over convenience or technological features. The primary caveat is the severely restricted membership eligibility—most consumers cannot join unless they live in one of 17 specific New Jersey towns or have a family member already in the credit union, making it irrelevant for the vast majority of potential members seeking financial services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: New Jersey Community — NCUA-insured credit union in MOORESTOWN, NJ. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
